title: SSE channel is recreated on reconnect, not reused, to discard stale messages from the aborted subtask
description: reconnect_sse aborts the old SSE task and creates a brand-new tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel rather than reusing the existing one. The comment explains why: without a new channel, a stale StreamEnded notification queued by the aborted task could be delivered on the new channel and trigger a double reconnect (abort + re-spawn loop). This is the correct design: (1) abort() does not guarantee that the task's final send() has not already been queued; (2) replacing sse_rx with a fresh receiver atomically drops all in-flight messages from the old task. A future contributor might suggest reusing the channel to avoid the allocation, or draining the old channel before re-using it. Draining is racy (a message can arrive after the drain loop); reusing without draining is the bug this design explicitly avoids. The reconnect_sse function's doc comment states the invariant that the caller must replace both sse_handle and sse_rx.
